lead: Drawing on extensive academic and professional expertise, Dr. Snigdha leads training and capacity-building programmes for government officials, development practitioners, academics, students, youth, and civil society actors, with a focus on gender equality, human rights, social inclusion, and rights-based policy and practice.

items.title: Empowering Rights: Women and Child Protection Training
items.subtitle: Regional Public Administration Training Centre (RPATC)
items.detail: Led training as a gender expert and trainer for government officials and development practitioners, focusing on human rights frameworks, national legal instruments, gender-based violence, child protection, referral mechanisms, and rights-based governance.

items.title: Meeting the SDGs: Poverty Reduction, Health, Education, and Gender Equality
items.subtitle: Bangladesh Public Administration Training Centre (BPATC)
items.detail: Delivered specialized training as a gender expert and trainer for mid- to senior-level government officers, policy planners, and administrators, focusing on the interconnections among SDGs 1, 3, 4, and 5; gender-responsive budgeting; inclusive policy planning; equity in service delivery; and climate resilience.

items.title: Feminism: Theoretical Waves and Local Transformations
items.subtitle: Pragroshor
items.detail: Facilitated the workshop as a gender expert and trainer for university students, faculty members, and gender activists, covering feminist theoretical waves, intersectionality, digital activism, and feminist movements in Bangladesh.

items.title: Youth Empowerment Training
items.subtitle: Pragroshor
items.detail: Conducted training as a gender expert and trainer for schoolchildren, adolescents, youth, feminists, and gender-rights actors, focusing on youth empowerment, gender equality, rights awareness, and meaningful youth engagement.

items.title: Training on Gender and Development
items.subtitle: Development Research Initiative (dRi)
items.detail: Delivered training as a gender expert and trainer for development professionals and NGO practitioners, focusing on gender equality, intersectional analysis, rights-based approaches, and the integration of feminist perspectives into program design and policy advocacy.
